violations.text: Pasta w/ Chicken was observed cooling in refrigeration in covered pans at 72 degrees Fahrenheit. Based on temperature and time of preparation, pans were uncovered to allow for rapid cooling at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2025

At the time of inspection, a large, covered plastic container of tomato sauce that was prepped/cooked previous day was observed at 52 degrees Fahrenheit in refrigeration. The tomato sauce was not cooled properly to 41 degrees Fahrenheit or below within the total of 6 hours from previous day. Tomato Sauce was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2025

At the time of inspection, several food items were observed cooling for later service in covered tin containers stacked on top of each other. Food items are to be arranged in the EQUIPMENT to provide maximum heat transfer through the container walls, and loosely covered, or uncovered if protected from overhead contamination. (Food items were separated and uncovered for rapid cooling at the time of inspection.) Proper cooling strategies were discussed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2025

Bowls are being used as scoops in containers of dry ingredients. Utensils used to dispense non-time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods such as sugar and flour must be stored in the food item with their handles above the top of the food within the container. Bowls were removed at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2025
